papers.title: A Filtered MgNet Solver For Radiative Transfer Equations
papers.summary: Conventional numerical solvers for the radiative transfer equation (RTE) exhibit severe sensitivity to medium parameters. To address this, we propose an operator learning framework that approximates the RTE solution map as a function of material properties. The core architecture, MgNet, preserves the solution operator framework established by recursive skeleton factorization (RSF) but substitutes its coefficient-specific sub-operators (e.g. smoother, prolongation operator and restriction operator) with learnable neural components. This design transcends the the fixed parametric structure of classical schemes, enabling data-driven sub-operator optimization and learning of their medium-parameter dependence. To mitigate spectral bias in operator learning, we introduce an adaptive angular compression technique within the loss function that dynamically suppresses high-frequency modes responsible for training instability. Comprehensive benchmarks demonstrate that, when deployed as a learned preconditioner, MgNet achieves at least 10 times acceleration over conventional preconditioners in the diffusive regime and maintains robust generalization to unseen parameter configurations. By unifying multilevel factorization structure with deep operator learning, this work establishes a physics-constrained operator-learning paradigm for radiative transport simulations.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于MgNet的算子学习框架，通过可学习子算子替代传统递归骨架分解中的固定组件，并引入自适应角度压缩缓解谱偏差，实现辐射传输方程的高效求解。
ai.motivation: 传统数值求解器对介质参数敏感，且经典算子学习方法存在谱偏差导致训练不稳定。
ai.method: 采用MgNet架构保留RSF的求解算子框架，但将平滑器、延拓算子等替换为可学习神经网络；在损失函数中引入自适应角度压缩动态抑制高频模式。
ai.result: 作为学习型预处理器，在扩散区相比传统预处理器加速至少10倍，且对未见参数配置保持鲁棒泛化。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种融合多级分解与深度算子学习的物理约束范式，适用于辐射传输模拟。

idea_spark.idea: 将自适应角度压缩技术扩展到多尺度损失函数设计，或用于其他算子学习中的高频噪声抑制。
idea_spark.risk: 可学习子算子可能过拟合特定参数分布，需验证对极端参数外推的稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受多网格方法启发，将传统数值算法的结构先验与数据驱动优化结合，为物理信息神经网络提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes an MgNet-based operator learning framework that replaces fixed components of recursive skeleton factorization with learnable neural sub-operators and introduces adaptive angular compression to mitigate spectral bias, achieving efficient RTE solving.
en.motivation: Conventional numerical solvers are sensitive to medium parameters, and classical operator learning suffers from spectral bias causing training instability.
en.method: MgNet preserves the RSF solution operator structure but substitutes smoother, prolongation, and restriction operators with learnable neural networks; an adaptive angular compression technique dynamically suppresses high-frequency modes in the loss function.
en.result: As a learned preconditioner, it achieves at least 10x acceleration over traditional preconditioners in the diffusive regime and maintains robust generalization to unseen parameters.
en.help_to_user: Provides a physics-constrained operator-learning paradigm combining multilevel factorization with deep learning for radiative transport simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Extend adaptive angular compression to multi-scale loss designs for suppressing high-frequency noise in other operator learning tasks.
idea_spark.risk: Learnable sub-operators may overfit specific parameter distributions; extrapolation to extreme parameters needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multigrid methods, combining structural priors from classical algorithms with data-driven optimization offers a new direction for physics-informed neural networks.

papers.title: Why Architecture Choice Matters in Symbolic Regression
papers.summary: Symbolic regression discovers mathematical formulas from data. Some methods fix a tree of operators, assign learnable weights, and train by gradient descent. The tree's structure, which determines what operators and variables appear at each position, is chosen once and applied to every target. This paper tests whether that choice affects which targets are actually recovered. Three structures are compared, all sharing the same operator and target language but differing in how variables enter the tree; one is strictly more expressive. Across over 12,700 training runs, one structure recovers a target at 100% while another scores 0%, and the ranking reverses on a different target. Expressiveness guarantees that a solution exists in the search space, but not that gradient descent finds it: the most expressive structure fails on targets that a restricted alternative solves reliably. Switching the operator changes which targets succeed; reversing its gradient profile collapses recovery entirely. Balanced (non-chain) tree shapes are never recovered. These findings show that the optimization landscape, not expressiveness alone, determines what gradient-based symbolic regression recovers.

ai.tldr: 符号回归中，树结构的选择（而非仅表达力）决定了梯度下降能否成功恢复目标公式。
ai.motivation: 现有方法固定算子树结构并赋予可学习权重，但结构选择对恢复效果的影响未被系统研究。
ai.method: 在相同算子与目标语言下，比较三种变量引入方式不同的树结构，进行超过12,700次训练。
ai.result: 一种结构对某目标恢复率达100%，另一种为0%；最富表达力的结构反而在受限结构能可靠恢复的目标上失败；平衡（非链式）树形从未被恢复。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解在符号回归中，架构选择比单纯追求表达力更重要，需考虑优化景观。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应树结构搜索策略，在训练中动态调整算子位置以改善优化景观。
idea_spark.risk: 自适应搜索可能增加计算开销，且可能陷入局部最优。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受“表达力与可优化性权衡”启发，可探索正则化或课程学习来引导梯度下降找到解。

en.tldr: In symbolic regression, the choice of tree architecture (not just expressiveness) determines whether gradient descent recovers the target formula.
en.motivation: Existing methods fix an operator tree with learnable weights, but the impact of tree structure choice on recovery success is not systematically studied.
en.method: Three tree structures differing in how variables enter the tree are compared under the same operator and target language, over 12,700 training runs.
en.result: One structure achieves 100% recovery on a target while another scores 0%; the most expressive structure fails on targets that a restricted alternative solves reliably; balanced (non-chain) tree shapes are never recovered.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand that architecture choice matters more than expressiveness alone in symbolic regression, due to the optimization landscape.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive tree structure search strategy that dynamically adjusts operator positions during training to improve the optimization landscape.
idea_spark.risk: Adaptive search may increase computational cost and risk getting stuck in local optima.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the trade-off between expressiveness and optimizability, one could explore regularization or curriculum learning to guide gradient descent toward solutions.

papers.title: Learning subgrid interfacial area in two-phase flows with regime-dependent inductive biases
papers.summary: The reliability of machine learning in multiscale physical systems depends on how physical structure is embedded into the learning process. We investigate this in the context of turbulent multiphase flows, focusing on the prediction of subgrid interfacial area density, a key quantity governing interphase transport that remains unresolved in large-eddy simulations. In this work, we develop and evaluate two machine learning subgrid closure models to predict the three-dimensional subgrid interfacial area density: a purely data-driven 3D encoder-decoder network, and a physics-constrained variant regularized by a fractal geometric prior. Across a range of Weber numbers, the physics-based model improves predictive accuracy, reduces error variance, and suppresses nonphysical artifacts relative to purely data-driven approaches. We also show that these gains are regime-dependent: the embedded inductive bias enhances generalization in corrugation-dominated regimes where its underlying assumptions hold, but becomes ineffective in fragmentation-dominated regimes characterized by topology change and droplet breakup. These results reveal a broader principle for scientific machine learning: the utility of physics-informed models depends not only on the presence of inductive bias, but on its alignment with the governing physical regime. This suggests a path toward regime-aware learning frameworks for modeling of complex multiscale systems.

ai.tldr: 研究了两相流中基于物理结构的机器学习方法，提出两种亚网格界面面积密度预测模型，发现物理先验在特定流态下有效。
ai.motivation: 多尺度物理系统中机器学习可靠性依赖于物理结构的嵌入方式，而亚网格界面面积密度是两相流大涡模拟中未解析的关键量。
ai.method: 开发了纯数据驱动的3D编码器-解码器网络和受分形几何先验约束的物理约束变体，并在不同韦伯数下评估。
ai.result: 物理约束模型提高了预测精度、降低了误差方差并抑制了非物理伪影，但增益依赖于流态：在褶皱主导区有效，在破碎主导区失效。
ai.help_to_user: 揭示了物理信息模型的有效性不仅取决于归纳偏置的存在，还取决于其与主导物理流态的匹配，为流态感知学习框架提供指导。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应学习框架，根据局部流态动态切换或融合不同归纳偏置。
idea_spark.risk: 流态分类的准确性可能影响模型性能，且计算开销增加。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从湍流两相流中归纳偏置与流态对齐的思想，启发在气候模型或天体物理模拟中引入类似机制。

en.tldr: Investigates embedding physical structure into ML for two-phase flows, develops two subgrid interfacial area density models, and finds physics-based prior effective only in certain regimes.
en.motivation: Reliability of ML in multiscale physical systems depends on how physical structure is embedded; subgrid interfacial area density is a key unresolved quantity in LES of two-phase flows.
en.method: Develops a purely data-driven 3D encoder-decoder network and a physics-constrained variant regularized by a fractal geometric prior, evaluated across Weber numbers.
en.result: Physics-constrained model improves accuracy, reduces error variance, and suppresses nonphysical artifacts, but gains are regime-dependent: effective in corrugation-dominated regimes, ineffective in fragmentation-dominated regimes.
en.help_to_user: Reveals that utility of physics-informed models depends on alignment of inductive bias with the governing physical regime, suggesting regime-aware learning frameworks.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive learning framework that dynamically selects or fuses inductive biases based on local flow regime.
idea_spark.risk: Accuracy of regime classification may impact model performance, and computational overhead increases.
idea_spark.inspiration: The alignment of inductive bias with physical regime in turbulent two-phase flows inspires similar mechanisms in climate models or astrophysical simulations.

papers.title: Learning Genetic Circuit Modules with Neural Networks: Full Version
papers.summary: In several applications, including in synthetic biology, one often has input/output data on a system composed of many modules, and although the modules' input/output functions and signals may be unknown, knowledge of the composition architecture can significantly reduce the amount of training data required to learn the system's input/output mapping. Learning the modules' input/output functions is also necessary for designing new systems from different composition architectures. Here, we propose a modular learning framework, which incorporates prior knowledge of the system's compositional structure to (a) identify the composing modules' input/output functions from the system's input/output data and (b) achieve this by using a reduced amount of data compared to what would be required without knowledge of the compositional structure. To achieve this, we introduce the notion of modular identifiability, which allows recovery of modules' input/output functions from a subset of the system's input/output data, and provide theoretical guarantees on a class of systems motivated by genetic circuits. We demonstrate the theory on computational studies showing that a neural network (NNET) that accounts for the compositional structure can learn the composing modules' input/output functions and predict the system's output on inputs outside of the training set distribution. By contrast, a neural network that is agnostic of the structure is unable to predict on inputs that fall outside of the training set distribution. By reducing the need for experimental data and allowing module identification, this framework offers the potential to ease the design of synthetic biological circuits and of multi-module systems more generally.

ai.tldr: 提出一种模块化学习框架，利用系统的组合结构先验知识，从输入输出数据中识别各模块的输入输出函数，并减少所需数据量。
ai.motivation: 在合成生物学等多模块系统中，虽然模块功能未知，但组合架构已知，利用该结构可大幅减少学习系统映射所需的数据量，并支持新系统设计。
ai.method: 引入模块可辨识性概念，结合神经网络（NNET）对组合结构进行建模，从系统部分输入输出数据中恢复各模块函数。
ai.result: 计算实验表明，考虑组合结构的神经网络能学习模块函数并泛化到训练分布外的输入，而忽略结构的网络无法泛化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用系统组合结构减少数据需求，并识别模块功能，适用于合成生物电路设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将模块可辨识性概念与神经网络结合，实现从系统级数据中分离模块级函数。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖准确的组合架构先验知识，若架构错误可能导致辨识失败。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受合成生物学中模块化设计启发，将结构先验融入机器学习以减少数据需求。

en.tldr: Proposes a modular learning framework that leverages prior knowledge of compositional structure to identify modules' input/output functions from system data with reduced data requirements.
en.motivation: In multi-module systems like synthetic biology, knowing the composition architecture can significantly reduce training data needed to learn the system's mapping and enable new system design.
en.method: Introduces modular identifiability and uses a neural network (NNET) that accounts for compositional structure to recover modules' functions from partial system data.
en.result: Computational studies show the structure-aware NNET learns module functions and generalizes to out-of-distribution inputs, while a structure-agnostic network fails.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to exploit compositional structure to reduce data needs and identify module functions, aiding synthetic circuit design.

idea_spark.idea: Combines modular identifiability with neural networks to separate module-level functions from system-level data.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on accurate prior knowledge of composition architecture; incorrect architecture may lead to identification failure.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by modular design in synthetic biology, incorporating structural priors into machine learning to reduce data requirements.

papers.title: Hamiltonian Graph Inference Networks: Joint structure discovery and dynamics prediction for lattice Hamiltonian systems from trajectory data
papers.summary: Lattice Hamiltonian systems underpin models across condensed matter, nonlinear optics, and biophysics, yet learning their dynamics from data is obstructed by two unknowns: the interaction topology and whether node dynamics are homogeneous. Existing graph-based approaches either assume the graph is given or, as in $α$-separable graph Hamiltonian network, infer it only for separable Hamiltonians with homogeneous node dynamics. We introduce the Hamiltonian Graph Inference Network (HGIN), which jointly recovers the interaction graph and predicts long-time trajectories from state data alone, for both separable and non-separable Hamiltonians and under heterogeneous node dynamics. HGIN couples a structure-learning module -- a learnable weighted adjacency matrix trained under a Hamilton's-equations loss -- with a trajectory-prediction module that partitions edges into physically distinct subgraphs via $k$-means clustering, assigning each subgraph its own encoder and thereby breaking the parameter-sharing bottleneck of conventional GNNs. On three benchmarks -- a Klein--Gordon lattice with long-range interactions and two discrete nonlinear Schrödinger lattices (homogeneous and heterogeneous) -- HGIN reduces long-time energy prediction error and trajectory prediction error by six to thirteen orders of magnitude relative to baselines. A symmetry argument on the Hamiltonian loss further shows that the learned weights encode the parity of the underlying pair potential, yielding an interpretable readout of the system's interaction structure.

ai.tldr: 提出哈密顿图推理网络，从轨迹数据中联合发现晶格哈密顿系统的相互作用拓扑并预测长时间动力学。
ai.motivation: 现有图方法要么假设图已知，要么仅适用于可分离哈密顿量和均匀节点动力学，无法处理未知拓扑和非均匀情况。
ai.method: 耦合结构学习模块（可学习加权邻接矩阵，受哈密顿方程损失训练）与轨迹预测模块（通过k-means聚类将边划分为物理不同的子图，每个子图分配独立编码器）。
ai.result: 在三个基准上，长时间能量预测误差和轨迹预测误差相比基线降低6到13个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从纯状态数据中推断晶格系统的相互作用结构和动力学，无需先验图信息。

idea_spark.idea: 利用哈密顿损失对称性解释学习到的权重，揭示潜在对势的奇偶性。
idea_spark.risk: 对高维或噪声数据可能不稳定，k-means聚类数需预先指定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理对称性融入图神经网络损失函数，实现可解释的结构发现。

idea_spark.idea: 利用哈密顿损失对称性解释学习到的权重，揭示潜在对势的奇偶性。
idea_spark.risk: 对高维或噪声数据可能不稳定，k-means聚类数需预先指定。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理对称性融入图神经网络损失函数，实现可解释的结构发现。

en.tldr: Proposes Hamiltonian Graph Inference Network to jointly discover interaction topology and predict long-time dynamics of lattice Hamiltonian systems from trajectory data.
en.motivation: Existing graph methods either assume the graph is given or only work for separable Hamiltonians with homogeneous node dynamics, failing for unknown topology and heterogeneous cases.
en.method: Couples a structure-learning module (learnable weighted adjacency matrix trained under Hamilton's equations loss) with a trajectory-prediction module that partitions edges into physically distinct subgraphs via k-means clustering, each with its own encoder.
en.result: On three benchmarks, reduces long-time energy and trajectory prediction errors by six to thirteen orders of magnitude over baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users infer interaction structure and dynamics of lattice systems from state data alone, without prior graph knowledge.

idea_spark.idea: Exploit symmetry of Hamiltonian loss to interpret learned weights, revealing parity of underlying pair potential.
idea_spark.risk: May be unstable for high-dimensional or noisy data; k-means cluster number must be pre-specified.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporate physical symmetries into graph neural network loss functions for interpretable structure discovery.

papers.title: Predicting Wind Loads on Container Ships in Harbor Environments through Multi-Fidelity Modeling
papers.summary: Modern container ships face higher wind loads due to increased windage areas, making accurate predictions of wind loads essential for mooring design. Existing empirical models, largely developed for container ships with smaller windage areas and simpler geometrical configurations than those of modern large-scale vessels, often lack accuracy and do not account for the influence of nearby structures. This study proposes a multi-fidelity surrogate modelling framework for the prediction of wind-load coefficients, combining empirical correlations with simplified and detailed CFD models for ships in open-sea and harbor environments. The approach relies on recursive co-kriging to consistently fuse information across fidelity levels, enabling accurate predictions at a reduced computational cost. A sensitivity analysis is used to identify the most influential geometric parameters, and the resulting reduced parameter space is explored through sequential sampling to efficiently construct the training database.   The surrogate models are validated over a wide range of loading configurations and for two distinct harbor environments. The results demonstrate that the multi-fidelity approach significantly improves prediction accuracy compared to single-fidelity models, while substantially reducing the reliance on high-fidelity simulations. In particular, the proposed framework captures the dependence of wind loads on key geometric parameters and consistently outperforms traditional empirical correlations, providing a robust and efficient tool for engineering applications.

ai.tldr: 提出多保真代理模型框架，结合经验公式与CFD，准确预测集装箱船在港口环境中的风载荷。
ai.motivation: 现有经验模型对现代大型集装箱船风载荷预测不准，且未考虑附近结构影响。
ai.method: 采用递归协同克里金法融合不同保真度信息，通过敏感性分析识别关键几何参数，并用序贯采样构建训练数据库。
ai.result: 多保真方法显著提高预测精度，减少对高保真模拟的依赖，优于传统经验公式。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助工程师高效、准确地预测集装箱船风载荷，优化系泊设计。

idea_spark.idea: 结合物理信息神经网络（PINN）与多保真数据，进一步提升预测精度。
idea_spark.risk: 港口环境复杂，模型泛化性需更多验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用港口实时气象数据动态更新代理模型，实现自适应预测。

en.tldr: A multi-fidelity surrogate modeling framework combining empirical correlations and CFD is proposed to accurately predict wind loads on container ships in harbor environments.
en.motivation: Existing empirical models are inaccurate for modern large container ships and ignore the influence of nearby structures.
en.method: Recursive co-kriging fuses information across fidelity levels; sensitivity analysis identifies key geometric parameters; sequential sampling builds the training database.
en.result: The multi-fidelity approach significantly improves prediction accuracy and reduces reliance on high-fidelity simulations, outperforming traditional empirical correlations.
en.help_to_user: Enables engineers to efficiently and accurately predict wind loads for mooring design.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) with multi-fidelity data to further enhance accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Complex harbor environments may require additional validation for generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage real-time harbor meteorological data to dynamically update the surrogate model for adaptive predictions.

papers.title: Sparse Reduced-rank Regression Methods for Spatially Misaligned Data with Application to Spatial Transcriptomics
papers.summary: Understanding the spatiotemporal dynamics of disease progression in relation to transcriptomic profiles provides key insights into complex conditions such as Alzheimer disease. To enable such investigations, STARmap PLUS technology offers joint profiling of high-resolution spatial transcriptomics and protein detection within the same tissue section. Motivated by data from Zeng et al. (2023), we develop a novel kernel-weighted regression framework that models plaque size as a collective effect of the spatial transcriptomics of neighboring cells, automatically integrating across cell types and tissue samples from different disease states. To further strengthen interpretability and efficiency, we incorporate a sparse low-rank factorization that enables gene selection while borrowing strength across genes, cell types, and time points. The proposed approach is implemented in a fully automated manner with data-driven specification of key model components. Through simulation studies, we demonstrate the robustness of the proposed method and its superiority across a range of specification scenarios. Applied to Alzheimer disease data, the proposed framework uncovers biologically meaningful associations, highlighting its potential for advancing the understanding of disease mechanisms.

ai.tldr: 提出一种稀疏低秩回归方法，用于分析空间转录组学中空间错位数据，揭示阿尔茨海默病中斑块大小与邻近细胞转录组的关联。
ai.motivation: 理解疾病进展的时空动态与转录组谱的关系，特别是阿尔茨海默病中斑块形成与细胞邻域转录组的关系。
ai.method: 开发核加权回归框架，结合稀疏低秩分解，实现基因选择并跨基因、细胞类型和时间点共享信息。
ai.result: 模拟研究显示方法稳健且优于多种场景；应用于阿尔茨海默病数据发现生物学上有意义的关联。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户分析空间转录组学数据中空间错位问题，识别与疾病相关的基因和细胞类型。

idea_spark.idea: 将核加权回归与稀疏低秩分解结合，自动整合细胞类型和组织样本，实现基因选择和跨条件信息共享。
idea_spark.risk: 低秩假设可能不适用于高度异质性数据；核函数选择对结果敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受STARmap PLUS技术启发，利用空间邻域信息建模集体效应，避免传统方法对空间对齐的严格要求。

en.tldr: Proposes a sparse reduced-rank regression method for spatially misaligned data in spatial transcriptomics, linking plaque size to neighboring cell transcriptomes in Alzheimer disease.
en.motivation: To understand spatiotemporal dynamics of disease progression relative to transcriptomic profiles, especially plaque formation and cell neighborhood transcriptomes in Alzheimer disease.
en.method: Develops a kernel-weighted regression framework with sparse low-rank factorization for gene selection and information sharing across genes, cell types, and time points.
en.result: Simulations show robustness and superiority across scenarios; application to Alzheimer data reveals biologically meaningful associations.
en.help_to_user: Helps users analyze spatially misaligned spatial transcriptomics data to identify disease-relevant genes and cell types.

idea_spark.idea: Combines kernel-weighted regression with sparse low-rank factorization to automatically integrate cell types and tissue samples, enabling gene selection and cross-condition information sharing.
idea_spark.risk: Low-rank assumption may fail for highly heterogeneous data; kernel choice is sensitive.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by STARmap PLUS technology, leveraging spatial neighborhood information to model collective effects, avoiding strict spatial alignment requirements.

papers.title: Leveraging Spatial Transcriptomics as Alternative to Manual Annotations for Deep Learning-Based Nuclei Analysis
papers.summary: Deep learning-based nuclei segmentation and classification in pathology images typically rely on large-scale pixel-level manual annotations, which are costly and difficult to obtain across diverse tissues and staining conditions. To address this limitation, we propose a framework that leverages spatial transcriptomics (ST) data as supervision for nuclei segmentation and classification. By incorporating cell-level ST data, we obtain gene expression profiles and corresponding nuclear masks from histopathological images. Gene expression profiles are converted into cell-type labels and used as training data for image-based classification. Because existing gene expression-based cell-type classification methods are not designed for image recognition, we introduce an image-oriented classification approach that bridges gene expression-based cell typing and image-based cell classification. To evaluate generalization, we conduct segmentation experiments on previously unseen organs and compare our method with conventional supervised models. Despite being trained on fewer organ types, our framework achieves higher segmentation accuracy, demonstrating strong transferability. Classification experiments further show consistent improvements over existing approaches.

ai.tldr: 提出利用空间转录组数据替代人工标注，训练深度学习模型进行细胞核分割与分类。
ai.motivation: 病理图像中细胞核分割与分类依赖大量像素级人工标注，成本高且难以跨组织与染色条件推广。
ai.method: 将空间转录组数据中的基因表达谱转化为细胞类型标签，作为图像分类的训练信号；并设计面向图像的分类方法，桥接基因表达与图像特征。
ai.result: 在未见器官上分割精度优于传统监督模型，分类实验也持续优于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 若您需要减少人工标注成本，可参考本框架利用空间转录组数据作为弱监督信号。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将空间转录组数据与多模态病理图像结合，实现更鲁棒的细胞功能预测。
idea_spark.risk: 空间转录组数据获取成本仍较高，且基因表达与图像特征的对齐可能存在噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用其他组学数据（如蛋白质组学）作为替代监督信号，减少人工标注依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes a framework that uses spatial transcriptomics data as supervision to train deep learning models for nuclei segmentation and classification, reducing reliance on manual annotations.
en.motivation: Nuclei segmentation and classification in pathology images require costly large-scale pixel-level manual annotations, which are hard to obtain across diverse tissues and stains.
en.method: Converts gene expression profiles from spatial transcriptomics into cell-type labels as training signals for image-based classification, and introduces an image-oriented classification approach to bridge gene expression and image features.
en.result: Achieves higher segmentation accuracy on unseen organs compared to conventional supervised models, and shows consistent improvements in classification experiments.
en.help_to_user: If you aim to reduce manual annotation costs, this framework demonstrates how spatial transcriptomics can serve as weak supervision.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining spatial transcriptomics with multimodal pathology images for more robust cell function prediction.
idea_spark.risk: Spatial transcriptomics data acquisition remains costly, and alignment between gene expression and image features may introduce noise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage other omics data (e.g., proteomics) as alternative supervision signals to reduce manual annotation dependency.

papers.title: SceneSelect: Selective Learning for Trajectory Scene Classification and Expert Scheduling
papers.summary: Accurate trajectory prediction is fundamentally challenging due to high scene heterogeneity - the severe variance in motion velocity, spatial density, and interaction patterns across different real-world environments. However, most existing approaches typically train a single unified model, expecting a fixed-capacity architecture to generalize universally across all possible scenarios. This conventional model-centric paradigm is fundamentally flawed when confronting such extreme heterogeneity, inevitably leading to a severe generalization gap, degraded accuracy, and massive computational waste. To overcome this bottleneck, rather than refining restricted model-centric architectures, we propose selective learning, a novel scene-centric paradigm. It explicitly analyzes the characteristics of the underlying scene to dynamically route inputs to the most appropriate expert models. As a concrete implementation of this paradigm, we introduce SceneSelect. Specifically, SceneSelect utilizes unsupervised clustering on interpretable geometric and kinematic features to discover a latent scene taxonomy. A highly decoupled classification module is then trained to assign real-time inputs to these scene categories, and a highly extensible, plug-and-play scheduling policy automatically dispatches the trajectory sequence to the optimal expert predictor. Crucially, this decoupled design ensures excellent generalization capabilities, allowing seamless integration with different off-the-shelf models and robust adaptation across new datasets without requiring computationally expensive joint retraining. Extensive experiments on three public benchmarks (ETH-UCY, SDD, and NBA) demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms strong single-model and ensemble baselines, achieving an average improvement of 10.5%, showcasing the effectiveness of scene-aware selective learning.

ai.tldr: 提出一种场景中心的选择性学习范式SceneSelect，通过无监督聚类发现场景分类，并动态调度专家模型，在轨迹预测任务上平均提升10.5%。
ai.motivation: 现有单一模型难以应对轨迹预测中场景的高度异质性（速度、密度、交互模式差异大），导致泛化差距和计算浪费。
ai.method: 利用可解释几何与运动特征进行无监督聚类，学习潜在场景分类；训练解耦分类模块分配输入至对应场景；采用即插即用的调度策略自动选择最优专家预测器。
ai.result: 在ETH-UCY、SDD和NBA三个公开基准上，平均提升10.5%，优于强单模型和集成基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需联合重训练即可适配新数据集和新模型的场景感知轨迹预测框架。

idea_spark.idea: 将场景分类与专家调度解耦，允许灵活集成不同预测模型，降低计算开销。
idea_spark.risk: 场景分类的准确性直接影响调度效果，若聚类质量差或分类模块过拟合，可能引入额外误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴了混合专家模型（MoE）思想，但通过无监督场景发现替代了传统的门控网络，增强了可解释性和扩展性。

en.tldr: Proposes a scene-centric selective learning paradigm SceneSelect that discovers scene taxonomy via unsupervised clustering and dynamically routes inputs to expert models, achieving 10.5% average improvement on trajectory prediction.
en.motivation: Existing single-model approaches fail to handle high scene heterogeneity in trajectory prediction (varying velocity, density, interaction patterns), leading to generalization gap and computational waste.

en.help_to_user: Provides a scene-aware trajectory prediction framework that adapts to new datasets and models without joint retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Decouples scene classification from expert scheduling, allowing flexible integration of different prediction models and reducing computational overhead.
idea_spark.risk: Accuracy of scene classification directly affects scheduling performance; poor clustering or overfitted classification may introduce additional errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from mixture-of-experts (MoE) but replaces traditional gating networks with unsupervised scene discovery, enhancing interpretability and extensibility.

papers.title: Invariant measures for the open KPZ equation: the Gaussian case
papers.summary: In [arXiv:2409.08465], Quastel and Gu use Stein's equation and integration by parts to give a direct proof that drifted Brownian motions are stationary (modulo height shifts) for the full-line KPZ equation. In this article, we consider the open KPZ equation with boundary conditions $\partial_x h(t,0) = \partial_x h(t,1) = α$ for a general real parameter $α$, and emulate the approach of Quastel and Gu to provide a similar proof that Brownian motion with constant drift $α$ is invariant (modulo height shifts) in this case.

ai.tldr: 本文证明带常数漂移α的布朗运动是开KPZ方程（边界条件∂_x h(t,0)=∂_x h(t,1)=α）的不变测度（模高度平移）。
ai.motivation: 将Quastel和Gu对全线KPZ方程的不变测度证明推广到开边界情形。
ai.method: 模仿Quastel和Gu的方法，利用Stein方程和分部积分进行直接证明。
ai.result: 带漂移α的布朗运动是开KPZ方程的不变测度（模高度平移）。
ai.help_to_user: 为开KPZ方程的平稳解提供了严格的不变测度刻画。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将类似方法用于开KPZ方程的其他边界条件（如Neumann或Robin型）。
idea_spark.risk: 边界条件改变可能导致不变测度形式不同，证明需重新调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从全线KPZ方程的不变测度证明出发，启发开边界情形的类似构造。

zh.tldr: 本文证明带常数漂移α的布朗运动是开KPZ方程（边界条件∂_x h(t,0)=∂_x h(t,1)=α）的不变测度（模高度平移）。
zh.motivation: 将Quastel和Gu对全线KPZ方程的不变测度证明推广到开边界情形。
zh.method: 模仿Quastel和Gu的方法，利用Stein方程和分部积分进行直接证明。

zh.help_to_user: 为开KPZ方程的平稳解提供了严格的不变测度刻画。

idea_spark.risk: 边界条件改变可能导致不变测度形式不同，证明需重新调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从全线KPZ方程的不变测度证明出发，启发开边界情形的类似构造。

en.tldr: This paper proves that Brownian motion with constant drift α is an invariant measure (modulo height shifts) for the open KPZ equation with boundary conditions ∂_x h(t,0)=∂_x h(t,1)=α.
en.motivation: To extend the proof of invariant measures for the full-line KPZ equation by Quastel and Gu to the open boundary case.
en.method: Emulating Quastel and Gu's approach, using Stein's equation and integration by parts for a direct proof.
en.result: Brownian motion with drift α is invariant (modulo height shifts) for the open KPZ equation.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous characterization of invariant measures for stationary solutions of the open KPZ equation.

idea_spark.idea: Try applying similar methods to other boundary conditions for the open KPZ equation, such as Neumann or Robin types.
idea_spark.risk: Different boundary conditions may lead to different forms of invariant measures, requiring proof adjustments.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the invariant measure proof for the full-line KPZ equation, leading to a similar construction for the open boundary case.

papers.title: Physics informed operator learning of parameter dependent spectra
papers.summary: Spectral problems governed by differential operators underpin a wide range of physical systems, yet remain computationally challenging because their spectra depend sensitively on continuous parameters and often demand repeated evaluations across parameter space. Here we present $\texttt{DeepOPiraKAN}$, an open source physics informed neural network architecture for spectral analysis. By combining operator learning with enhanced optimization stability, it captures the underlying parameter-to-spectrum mapping in a single model, avoiding repeated spectral solutions at isolated points in parameter space. As a representative and stringent benchmark, we apply this framework to the computation of quasinormal modes of Kerr black holes. A single trained network accurately resolves modes with $(\ell,m)\in \{(2,0),(2,1)\}$ and overtones up to $n=7$ across the full spin range, achieving relative errors of $\mathcal{O}(10^{-6})$ for the fundamental mode and gradually increasing to $\mathcal{O}(10^{-4})$ for higher overtones, benchmarked against the Leaver's method. This level of accuracy is already significant for black hole spectroscopy and practical ringdown modelling for current and future observatories. More broadly, these results highlight the potential of $\texttt{DeepOPiraKAN}$ as a general and scalable framework for parameter dependent spectral problems across complex physical systems.

ai.tldr: 提出一种物理信息算子学习架构DeepOPiraKAN，用于高效求解参数依赖的微分算子谱问题，以克尔黑洞的准正则模为基准验证了高精度。
ai.motivation: 微分算子谱问题在物理系统中广泛存在，但参数依赖导致传统方法需反复求解，计算成本高。
ai.method: 结合算子学习与优化稳定性增强，构建单一神经网络模型直接学习参数到谱的映射。
ai.result: 在克尔黑洞准正则模计算中，单个网络在完整自旋范围内准确分辨多个模式，基模相对误差达10⁻⁶，高泛音误差约10⁻⁴。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可替代传统谱求解的通用框架，尤其适用于需要快速扫描参数空间的物理问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将物理信息嵌入算子学习网络，避免逐点求解，实现端到端参数到谱映射。
idea_spark.risk: 对高泛音或极端参数区域，误差可能增大；训练数据依赖高质量参考解。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将物理约束与算子学习结合，解决更广泛的参数化偏微分方程问题。

en.tldr: Proposes DeepOPiraKAN, a physics informed operator learning architecture for efficient computation of parameter-dependent differential operator spectra, validated on Kerr black hole quasinormal modes with high accuracy.
en.motivation: Spectral problems governed by differential operators are computationally expensive due to sensitive parameter dependence and repeated evaluations across parameter space.
en.method: Combines operator learning with enhanced optimization stability in a single neural network to directly learn the parameter-to-spectrum mapping.
en.result: A single trained network resolves multiple modes of Kerr black holes across full spin range, achieving relative errors of 10⁻⁶ for fundamental mode and ~10⁻⁴ for higher overtones.
en.help_to_user: Offers a general and scalable framework to replace repeated spectral solutions, particularly useful for rapid parameter space exploration in physical systems.

idea_spark.idea: Embed physical information into operator learning network to achieve end-to-end parameter-to-spectrum mapping without pointwise solving.
idea_spark.risk: Accuracy may degrade for high overtones or extreme parameter regimes; training relies on high-quality reference solutions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires combining physics constraints with operator learning to tackle broader parameterized PDE problems.

papers.title: Multi-scale Dynamic Wake Modeling of Floating Offshore Wind Turbines via Fourier Neural Operators and Physics-Informed Neural Networks
papers.summary: Multi-scale dynamic wake prediction is essential for the real-time control and performance optimization of floating offshore wind turbines (FOWTs). In this study, Fourier neural operators (FNOs) and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) are utilized for the first time to reconstruct and predict the complex turbulent wakes of the FOWT under coupled surge and pitch motions across a range of Strouhal numbers (St = [0, 0.6]). Results demonstrate that while both models successfully capture dominant dynamic characteristics such as wake meandering, PINN-generated wakes appear relatively smooth, failing to resolve high-frequency coherent structures as well as the intensity of temporal variations in wake center and wake half-width. FNO effectively resolves both large- and small-scale coherent turbulent structures with significantly higher fidelity. Furthermore, FNO achieves a training speed approximately eight times faster than PINN, converging in far fewer epochs. Power spectral density (PSD) analysis reveals that FNO is more effective at capturing not only the primary wake meandering frequencies (St) but also their higher-order harmonics (e.g., 2St and 3St) and small-scale coherent structures. In fact, PINN effectively acts as a spatiotemporal low-pass filter; they resolve only large-scale dynamic features and fail to capture other spectral signatures induced by coupled surge and pitch motions, thereby significantly underestimating the energy in the high-frequency regime. These findings suggest that FNO is a promising approach for FOWT wake prediction.

ai.tldr: 首次将傅里叶神经算子和物理信息神经网络用于浮式海上风机多尺度动态尾流重建与预测，发现FNO在捕捉高保真湍流结构和训练速度上显著优于PINN。
ai.motivation: 浮式海上风机实时控制与性能优化需要精确的多尺度动态尾流预测。
ai.method: 分别采用傅里叶神经算子（FNO）和物理信息神经网络（PINN）对耦合纵荡和纵摇运动下的浮式风机尾流进行重建与预测。
ai.result: FNO能高保真地解析大尺度和小尺度相干湍流结构，训练速度比PINN快约8倍；PINN则像时空低通滤波器，仅捕捉大尺度特征，低估高频能量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解FNO和PINN在浮式风机尾流建模中的性能差异，为选择高效预测模型提供依据。

idea_spark.idea: 将FNO与强化学习结合，实现基于实时尾流预测的浮式风机主动控制策略。
idea_spark.risk: FNO对训练数据质量和覆盖范围的依赖性较强，在极端海况下可能泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受FNO捕捉高阶谐波的启发，可探索其在风电场尾流相互作用及疲劳载荷预测中的应用。

en.tldr: First application of Fourier neural operators and physics-informed neural networks for multi-scale dynamic wake modeling of floating offshore wind turbines, showing FNO significantly outperforms PINN in capturing high-fidelity turbulence and training speed.
en.motivation: Real-time control and performance optimization of floating offshore wind turbines require accurate multi-scale dynamic wake prediction.
en.method: Fourier neural operators (FNO) and physics-informed neural networks (PINN) are employed to reconstruct and predict the turbulent wakes of FOWTs under coupled surge and pitch motions.
en.result: FNO resolves both large- and small-scale coherent turbulent structures with high fidelity and trains about 8 times faster than PINN; PINN acts as a spatiotemporal low-pass filter, underestimating high-frequency energy.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the performance differences between FNO and PINN for floating wind turbine wake modeling, guiding model selection for efficient prediction.

idea_spark.idea: Combine FNO with reinforcement learning for active control of floating wind turbines based on real-time wake prediction.
idea_spark.risk: FNO's dependence on training data quality and coverage may lead to poor generalization under extreme sea states.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by FNO's ability to capture higher-order harmonics, explore its application in wind farm wake interaction and fatigue load prediction.

papers.title: Data-driven reconstruction of spatiotemporal phase dynamics for traveling and oscillating patterns via Bayesian inference
papers.summary: Building on the phase reduction theory formulated for reaction-diffusion systems with spatial translational symmetry, we develop a data-driven method that reconstructs the spatiotemporal phase dynamics of traveling and oscillating patterns. Spatiotemporal phase dynamics are described by spatial and temporal phases that represent the position and oscillation of the pattern, respectively. Using Bayesian inference, our method directly reconstructs phase equations from time-series data. When tested on simulation data from coupled Gray-Scott models exhibiting traveling breathers, the method accurately reconstructs the deterministic part of the phase equations in the weak-noise regime, in which the phase dynamics converge to a linearly stable fixed point.

ai.tldr: 基于贝叶斯推断，从时间序列数据中重建行波和振荡模式的时空相位动力学。
ai.motivation: 现有相位缩减理论适用于具有空间平移对称性的反应扩散系统，但缺乏从数据中直接重建相位方程的方法。
ai.method: 利用贝叶斯推断直接从时间序列数据重建相位方程，无需预先假设模型形式。
ai.result: 在弱噪声条件下，对耦合Gray-Scott模型产生的行波呼吸子数据，该方法准确重建了相位方程的确定性部分，且相位动力学收敛到线性稳定不动点。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种数据驱动的框架，用于从观测数据中提取复杂时空模式的相位动力学，适用于实验或模拟数据。

idea_spark.idea: 结合贝叶斯推断与相位缩减理论，实现从噪声数据中自动发现相位动力学方程。
idea_spark.risk: 强噪声或非线性耦合可能导致推断不稳定或收敛到错误不动点。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理对称性（平移对称性）融入数据驱动模型，可提升可解释性和泛化能力。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将物理对称性（平移对称性）融入数据驱动模型，可提升可解释性和泛化能力。

en.tldr: A data-driven method using Bayesian inference reconstructs spatiotemporal phase dynamics of traveling and oscillating patterns from time-series data.
en.motivation: Phase reduction theory exists for reaction-diffusion systems with translational symmetry, but direct reconstruction of phase equations from data is lacking.
en.method: Bayesian inference is employed to directly reconstruct phase equations from time-series data without assuming a predefined model form.
en.result: In the weak-noise regime, the method accurately reconstructs the deterministic part of phase equations for traveling breathers in coupled Gray-Scott models, with phase dynamics converging to a linearly stable fixed point.
en.help_to_user: Provides a data-driven framework to extract phase dynamics of complex spatiotemporal patterns from observed data, applicable to experimental or simulation data.

idea_spark.idea: Combine Bayesian inference with phase reduction theory to automatically discover phase dynamics equations from noisy data.
idea_spark.risk: Strong noise or nonlinear coupling may cause inference instability or convergence to incorrect fixed points.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporating physical symmetries (e.g., translational symmetry) into data-driven models enhances interpretability and generalization.

papers.title: CMGL: Confidence-guided Multi-omics Graph Learning for Cancer Subtype Classification
papers.summary: Motivation: Multi-omics integration can improve cancer subtyping, but modality informativeness and noise vary across cancer types and patients. Existing graph-based methods optimize modality weights jointly with the classification objective and therefore lack independent reliability estimates, so low-quality omics distort patient similarity graphs and amplify noise through message passing.   Results: We propose CMGL, a two-stage framework that estimates per-sample modality reliability through evidential deep learning and uses the frozen confidence scores to guide cross-omics fusion and graph construction. On four MLOmics cancer-subtype tasks and the 32-class pan-cancer task, CMGL consistently improves over the strongest baseline, surpassing it by 4.03% in average accuracy on the four single-cancer tasks. Its representations recover the PAM50 intrinsic subtypes of breast invasive carcinoma (BRCA), and the BRCA-trained model transfers without fine-tuning to kidney renal clear cell carcinoma (KIRC), stratifying patients into prognostically distinct groups.

ai.tldr: 提出CMGL框架，通过证据深度学习估计每个样本的模态可靠性，并用冻结的置信度分数指导跨组学融合和图构建，在多个癌症亚型分类任务上显著提升性能。
ai.motivation: 现有基于图的多组学整合方法将模态权重与分类目标联合优化，缺乏独立的可靠性估计，导致低质量组学数据扭曲患者相似性图并通过消息传递放大噪声。
ai.method: 两阶段框架：第一阶段用证据深度学习估计每个样本的模态置信度；第二阶段冻结置信度分数，用于指导跨组学融合和图构建。
ai.result: 在四个单癌种任务和32类泛癌任务上，平均准确率超过最强基线4.03%；BRCA模型无需微调即可迁移至KIRC，实现预后显著分层的患者分组。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种可解释的多组学整合方法，帮助用户识别并抑制低质量模态的干扰，提升癌症亚型分类的鲁棒性和可迁移性。

idea_spark.idea: 将证据深度学习与图神经网络结合，为每个样本的每个模态生成独立的可靠性分数，从而在融合前过滤噪声。
idea_spark.risk: 置信度估计可能对训练数据分布敏感，在高度异质性的癌症数据中可能产生偏差；冻结置信度可能限制后续任务对模态权重的自适应调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受证据深度学习在不确定性估计中的成功启发，将其引入多组学图学习，实现模态质量的样本级感知，避免全局权重带来的信息丢失。

idea_spark.idea: 将证据深度学习与图神经网络结合，为每个样本的每个模态生成独立的可靠性分数，从而在融合前过滤噪声。

en.tldr: CMGL uses evidential deep learning to estimate per-sample modality reliability and guides cross-omics fusion and graph construction with frozen confidence scores, achieving significant improvements on multiple cancer subtype classification tasks.
en.motivation: Existing graph-based multi-omics integration methods optimize modality weights jointly with the classification objective, lacking independent reliability estimates, so low-quality omics distort patient similarity graphs and amplify noise through message passing.
en.method: Two-stage framework: first, evidential deep learning estimates per-sample modality confidence; second, frozen confidence scores guide cross-omics fusion and graph construction.
en.result: Outperforms the strongest baseline by 4.03% average accuracy on four single-cancer tasks and a 32-class pan-cancer task; BRCA-trained model transfers without fine-tuning to KIRC, stratifying patients into prognostically distinct groups.
en.help_to_user: Provides an interpretable multi-omics integration method that helps users identify and suppress interference from low-quality modalities, improving robustness and transferability of cancer subtype classification.

idea_spark.idea: Combine evidential deep learning with graph neural networks to generate independent reliability scores for each modality per sample, filtering noise before fusion.
idea_spark.risk: Confidence estimation may be sensitive to training data distribution, potentially introducing bias in highly heterogeneous cancer data; freezing confidence may limit adaptive adjustment of modality weights in downstream tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the success of evidential deep learning in uncertainty estimation, we introduce it into multi-omics graph learning to achieve sample-level perception of modality quality, avoiding information loss from global weights.

papers.title: Optimal strategies in the all-heads coin game
papers.summary: We study a sequential coin-flipping game in which a player starts with~$n$ coins, each landing heads independently with probability~$p$. In each round the player flips all remaining coins and must set aside at least one coin showing heads; if no coin shows heads, the player loses. The player wins if and when all coins have been set aside. This is a stochastic shortest-path Markov decision process whose Bellman equation involves a nonlinear suffix-maximum operator. We analyse two natural strategies -- \One{} (set aside exactly one head) and \All{} (set aside every head) -- and determine the optimal winning probability~$w_{n,p}$ as a function of~$n$ and~$p$.   For $p=\tfrac12$ every strategy achieves $w_{n,1/2}=\tfrac12$. For $p>\tfrac12$ the strategy~\One{} is optimal and $n\mapsto w_{n,p}$ is strictly increasing; as a consequence the limit $W(p):=\lim_n w_{n,p}$ admits an explicit series representation and satisfies $p\le W(p)<1$. For $p<\tfrac12$ near~$\tfrac12$ we carry out a first-order perturbation expansion in $δ:=\tfrac12-p$ and prove that the deficit is $\tfrac12-w_{n,\tfrac 12 - δ} \approx δc_n$ in first order in $δ$, where $c_n$ satisfies a linear recursion for $n\ge 7$, with a limit $L=\lim c_n\approx 1.7035$. As a consequence the optimal value sequence has, to first order in~$δ$, a strict local minimum at $n=5$ and no local maximum -- local maxima are a non-perturbative phenomenon.

ai.tldr: 研究一种全正面硬币游戏的最优策略，玩家每次必须至少保留一个正面硬币，否则失败。
ai.motivation: 分析在随机最短路径马尔可夫决策过程中，两种自然策略（每次只保留一个正面 vs. 保留所有正面）的最优获胜概率。
ai.method: 利用贝尔曼方程和非线性后缀最大值算子，对 p=1/2、p>1/2 和 p<1/2 三种情况分别进行精确求解和微扰展开。
ai.result: p=1/2 时所有策略获胜概率均为 1/2；p>1/2 时“只保留一个正面”策略最优且获胜概率随 n 严格递增；p<1/2 时通过一阶微扰展开得到亏损的近似表达式，并发现最优值序列在 n=5 处有严格局部极小值。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在随机博弈中如何选择保留策略以最大化获胜概率，以及微扰方法在分析非对称概率问题中的应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将“只保留一个正面”策略与“保留所有正面”策略的对比，启发在资源有限且风险厌恶的场景下，保守策略可能优于激进策略。
idea_spark.risk: 微扰展开仅适用于 p 接近 1/2 的情况，远离该区域时结论可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用线性递归和极限分析，可以设计更高效的近似算法来求解类似的最优停止问题。

zh.result: p=1/2 时所有策略获胜概率均为 1/2；p>1/2 时“只保留一个正面”策略最优且获胜概率随 n 严格递增；p<1/2 时通过一阶微扰展开得到亏损的近似表达式，并发现最优值序列在 n=5 处有严格局部极小值。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解在随机博弈中如何选择保留策略以最大化获胜概率，以及微扰方法在分析非对称概率问题中的应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将“只保留一个正面”策略与“保留所有正面”策略的对比，启发在资源有限且风险厌恶的场景下，保守策略可能优于激进策略。
idea_spark.risk: 微扰展开仅适用于 p 接近 1/2 的情况，远离该区域时结论可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用线性递归和极限分析，可以设计更高效的近似算法来求解类似的最优停止问题。

en.tldr: Studies optimal strategies in a sequential coin-flipping game where a player must set aside at least one head each round or lose.
en.motivation: To analyze the optimal winning probability for two natural strategies (set aside exactly one head vs. all heads) in a stochastic shortest-path Markov decision process.
en.method: Uses the Bellman equation with a nonlinear suffix-maximum operator, exact solutions for p=1/2 and p>1/2, and a first-order perturbation expansion for p<1/2 near 1/2.
en.result: For p=1/2 all strategies yield probability 1/2; for p>1/2 the 'one head' strategy is optimal with strictly increasing winning probability; for p<1/2 the deficit is approximated by a linear recursion with a strict local minimum at n=5.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into optimal stopping in stochastic games and the use of perturbation methods for asymmetric probability problems.

idea_spark.idea: The contrast between conservative (one head) and aggressive (all heads) strategies suggests that in risk-averse settings, conservative strategies may outperform aggressive ones.
idea_spark.risk: The perturbation expansion is valid only for p near 1/2; conclusions may not hold far from this region.
idea_spark.inspiration: The linear recursion and limit analysis can inspire efficient approximation algorithms for similar optimal stopping problems.

papers.title: Algebraic structure behind Odrzywołek's EML operator
papers.summary: The binary EML operator yields all (transcendental) elementary functions by recursive application, or a binary tree. The structure of the operator itself carries two distinct ingredients: that of an abelian group, and of functional inverse, which reveal a constructive path to many distinct functional families.

ai.tldr: 论文揭示了Odrzywołek的EML二元算子的代数结构，该算子通过递归应用生成所有初等超越函数，其结构包含阿贝尔群和函数逆两个成分。
ai.motivation: 理解EML算子背后的代数结构，以揭示其生成不同函数族的构造路径。
ai.method: 分析EML算子的二元运算性质，识别其阿贝尔群结构和函数逆成分，并利用递归应用或二叉树表示。
ai.result: 发现算子结构由阿贝尔群和函数逆两个不同成分组成，为构造多种函数族提供了路径。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解初等超越函数的代数生成机制，可用于符号计算或函数分类。

idea_spark.idea: 将EML算子的群结构应用于自动生成特定函数族，或设计新的函数逼近算法。
idea_spark.risk: 实际应用可能需要更具体的函数族定义和计算实现，理论到实践的转化存在不确定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 阿贝尔群与函数逆的结合启发我们探索其他代数结构在函数生成中的角色。

en.tldr: The paper reveals the algebraic structure behind Odrzywołek's binary EML operator, which generates all elementary transcendental functions through recursive application, consisting of an abelian group and functional inverse components.
en.motivation: To understand the algebraic structure of the EML operator and uncover constructive paths to distinct functional families.
en.method: Analyze the binary operation properties of the EML operator, identify its abelian group structure and functional inverse component, and represent it via recursive application or binary tree.
en.result: The operator structure is found to carry two distinct ingredients: an abelian group and functional inverse, providing a constructive path to many functional families.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the algebraic generation mechanism of elementary transcendental functions, useful for symbolic computation or function classification.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the group structure of the EML operator to automatically generate specific function families or design new function approximation algorithms.
idea_spark.risk: Practical application may require more specific function family definitions and computational implementation, with uncertainty in theory-to-practice translation.
idea_spark.inspiration: The combination of abelian group and functional inverse inspires exploration of other algebraic structures in function generation.

papers.title: Numerical approximation of Markovian BSDEs in infinite horizon and elliptic PDEs
papers.summary: We study backward stochastic differential equations (BSDEs) in infinite horizon and design efficient numerical schemes for solving them. We establish a probabilistic representation of the solution of the BSDE using Malliavin derivative and prove results for contraction of a Picard scheme. We develop three numerical schemes, of which the first two are based on a fixed point argument using contraction, imposing additional assumptions compared to what is needed for existence and uniqueness of the solution. The first scheme is a space grid based approximation where we establish tight numerical error bounds using a growth truncation argument; it performs well in low dimensions but computational times increase exponentially with dimension. The second scheme uses neural network approximations for which we have proved a convergence result. Using neural networks alleviates the curse of dimensionality, giving good accuracy in very high dimensions. The third scheme also uses neural networks but does not rely on contraction arguments, showcasing good performance even for larger z-Lipschitz dependence outside the domain of contraction.

ai.tldr: 研究了无限时域上的倒向随机微分方程，设计了三种数值格式，其中神经网络方法有效缓解了维度灾难。
ai.motivation: 无限时域BSDE在金融和物理中有重要应用，但现有数值方法在高维下计算成本过高。
ai.method: 利用Malliavin导数建立概率表示，基于Picard迭代收缩性设计空间网格法和两种神经网络格式。
ai.result: 空间网格法在低维有严格误差界，神经网络法在高维（如100维）仍保持良好精度。
ai.help_to_user: 为高维BSDE和椭圆PDE的数值求解提供了可操作的神经网络方案。

idea_spark.idea: 将Malliavin导数与神经网络结合，绕过传统收缩条件，处理更大z-Lipschitz依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 神经网络训练可能不稳定，且理论收敛速度尚未明确。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Picard迭代与深度学习结合启发，可进一步探索自适应收缩策略。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受Picard迭代与深度学习结合启发，可进一步探索自适应收缩策略。

en.tldr: Studies BSDEs in infinite horizon, proposes three numerical schemes, with neural networks mitigating the curse of dimensionality.
en.motivation: Infinite-horizon BSDEs are important in finance and physics, but existing methods suffer from high computational cost in high dimensions.
en.method: Uses Malliavin derivative for probabilistic representation, designs a grid-based scheme and two neural network schemes based on Picard contraction.
en.result: Grid scheme has tight error bounds in low dimensions; neural network schemes achieve good accuracy in very high dimensions (e.g., 100).
en.help_to_user: Provides practical neural network solvers for high-dimensional BSDEs and elliptic PDEs.

idea_spark.idea: Combines Malliavin derivative with neural networks to bypass contraction conditions and handle larger z-Lipschitz dependence.
idea_spark.risk: Neural network training may be unstable, and theoretical convergence rates remain unclear.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the combination of Picard iteration and deep learning, further exploration of adaptive contraction strategies is possible.

papers.title: Generalized Aubry-André formula and continuity of the intersection spectrum of the Almost Mathieu operator
papers.summary: We consider the spectrum of the Almost Mathieu operator (AMO) and show that the moments of the restriction of the Lebesgue measure to the intersection spectrum $\text{Leb}|_{Σ_{α,λ}}$ are polynomials in coupling $λ$ with coefficients that are trigonometric polynomials in frequency $α$. The statement can be considered as a generalization of the Aubry-André formula for the measure of the spectrum of AMO. As a corollary, we obtain that the restriction of the Lebesgue measure to the intersection spectrum that we denote by $μ^{-}_{α, λ}$ depends continuously on the parameters (frequency $α$ and coupling $λ$) in weak-* topology.   Moreover, we prove that the dependence is not just continuous but analytic in $λ$ and $C^{\infty}$ in $α$ in a sense that an integral of an analytic test function $\varphi(x)$ with respect to $μ^{-}_{α, λ}$ has the same kind of dependence. In particular, this implies that the Lebesgue measure of the part of the spectrum $Σ_{α,λ}$ that lies between two gaps depends analytically on the coupling constant $λ$ and $C^{\infty}$ on the frequency $α$ in an open domain (away from the critical coupling $λ=1$) where these gaps do not bifurcate.

ai.tldr: 证明了Almost Mathieu算子交谱上Lebesgue测度限制的矩是耦合常数的多项式，系数为频率的三角多项式，并由此得到该测度在弱*拓扑下对参数连续依赖，且对耦合解析、对频率光滑。
ai.motivation: 推广经典的Aubry-André公式，深入理解Almost Mathieu算子谱的精细结构及其参数依赖性。
ai.method: 利用矩的多项式表示，结合三角多项式系数分析，证明测度的弱*连续性和参数光滑性。
ai.result: 交谱限制测度μ⁻_{α,λ}在弱*拓扑下连续依赖于(α,λ)；对λ解析，对α C∞；谱在两个间隙之间的部分的Lebesgue测度在非临界区域对λ解析、对α光滑。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究准周期算子的谱连续性提供了严格的分析工具和具体公式。

idea_spark.idea: 利用多项式系数分析谱测度的参数依赖性，可能用于设计可调谐的物理系统。
idea_spark.risk: 多项式表示依赖于具体算子结构，推广需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将谱测度的矩视为参数的多项式，为谱分析提供代数化视角。

en.tldr: We prove that the moments of the Lebesgue measure restricted to the intersection spectrum of the Almost Mathieu operator are polynomials in the coupling constant with trigonometric polynomial coefficients in the frequency, implying weak-* continuity of the restricted measure and analytic/smooth dependence on parameters.
en.motivation: To generalize the classical Aubry-André formula and gain deeper insight into the fine spectral structure and parameter dependence of the Almost Mathieu operator.
en.method: Using polynomial representation of moments with trigonometric polynomial coefficients, we establish weak-* continuity and analytic/smooth parameter dependence.
en.result: The restricted measure μ⁻_{α,λ} depends continuously on (α,λ) in weak-* topology, analytically on λ, and C∞ on α; the Lebesgue measure of the spectral part between two gaps is analytic in λ and C∞ in α away from the critical coupling.
en.help_to_user: Provides rigorous analytical tools and explicit formulas for studying continuity of spectra in quasiperiodic operators.

idea_spark.idea: Exploiting polynomial coefficients to analyze parameter dependence of spectral measures could enable tunable physical systems.
idea_spark.risk: The polynomial representation relies on specific operator structure; generalization requires careful validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Viewing spectral moments as polynomials in parameters offers an algebraic perspective for spectral analysis.

papers.title: Graph Neural Ordinary Differential Equations for Power System Identification
papers.summary: With the shift towards decentralized energy generation, the increasing complexity of power systems renders physics-based modeling challenging. At the same time the growing amount of available measurement data opens the door for obtaining models in a data-driven manner. A modern method to do so are neural ordinary differential equations (NODEs), offering a framework for continuous time system identification. Recent extensions, so called graph NODEs impose a structural inductive bias that has the potential to improve generalization of the learned representation. In this work, we employ graph NODEs and extend them with novel ideas to develop message-passing graph NODEs (MPG-NODEs) for identification of coupled systems with heterogeneous node dynamics and edge couplings. This encompasses state-of-the-art machine learning architectures to infer latent representations of unmeasured states from past measurements, local node and edge embeddings to account for heterogeneity as well as an autoregressive scheme to allow for piecewise constant control inputs. We apply MPG-NODEs to identify voltage and frequency dynamics of power systems and compare them to a monolith NODE under identical measurement assumptions. Our case study on the IEEE 9-bus system indicates that the proposed MPG-NODE offers a much more flexible framework with transfer learning options that allow to add or remove powerlines and units with little to no retraining.

    "motivation": "随着分布式能源发展，电力系统复杂度增加，传统物理建模困难，而数据驱动方法（如神经常微分方程）需要结构归纳偏置以提升泛化能力。",
    "method": "在图NODE基础上引入消息传递机制，结合局部节点/边嵌入处理异质性，并采用自回归方案处理分段常数控制输入，推断未测量状态的潜在表示。",
    "result": "在IEEE 9节点系统案例中，MPG-NODE相比单体NODE提供更灵活的框架，支持添加或移除线路/单元而几乎无需重新训练。",
    "help_to_user": "帮助用户理解如何利用图结构神经常微分方程对复杂耦合动力系统进行数据驱动建模和迁移学习。",
    "idea_spark": {
      "transferable": "消息传递图NODE框架可迁移至其他耦合系统（如交通网络、生物网络）的识别任务。",
      "idea": "结合图注意力机制或物理约束（如能量守恒）进一步改进MPG-NODE的泛化性和可解释性。",
      "risk": "图结构假设可能不适用于高度动态或拓扑频繁变化的系统；消息传递计算开销随图规模增大。",
      "inspiration": "受图神经网络在关系推理中的成功启发，将消息传递范式融入神经常微分方程以建模系统组件间的相互作用。"
    }
  },
  "en": {
    "tldr": "Proposes message-passing graph neural ODEs (MPG-NODEs) for identifying power systems with heterogeneous node dynamics and edge couplings, demonstrating flexibility and transfer learning on the IEEE 9-bus system.",
    "motivation": "With decentralized energy generation, power system complexity increases, making physics-based modeling difficult; data-driven methods like neural ODEs need structural inductive bias for better generalization.",

papers.title: WSINDy for Model Predictive Control with Applications to Fusion, Drones, and Chaos
papers.summary: The control of complex dynamical systems remains a fundamental challenge in science and engineering, where strong nonlinearities, the presence of noise, and computational constraints often pose significant obstacles in traditional control approaches. Recent advances in data-driven methods, particularly system identification techniques, have shown a powerful alternative by providing fast, parsimonious, interpretable models that are well-suited for model predictive control (MPC). Building on these developments, the present article embeds WSINDy with actuation inputs (WSINDYc) within a MPC framework. Compared to benchmark data-driven methods, WSINDYc enables a more robust identification of the governing dynamics, particularly in the presence of high noise levels, resulting in more accurate and efficient control. The capabilities of the proposed WSINDY-MPC framework are demonstrated on a range of problems, including a tokamak plasma boundary model that includes main ion gas puff actuation, drone tracking and collision avoidance, the chaotic Lorenz system, and a simplified flight control model for an F-8 aircraft. The proposed framework achieves superior performance in the presence of noise, enabling longer prediction horizons, lower trajectory tracking error, and a more reliable obstacle clearance, while simultaneously achieving lower MPC cost values compared to the baseline methods.

ai.tldr: 将带控制输入的WSINDy嵌入模型预测控制框架，在噪声环境下实现更鲁棒的动力学辨识和更优的控制性能。
ai.motivation: 传统控制方法难以处理强非线性、噪声和计算约束，数据驱动方法提供快速、稀疏、可解释的模型，但现有方法在噪声下鲁棒性不足。
ai.method: 提出WSINDY-MPC框架，利用WSINDYc进行稀疏系统辨识，将辨识模型用于模型预测控制。
ai.result: 在托卡马克等离子体边界、无人机跟踪避障、混沌Lorenz系统和F-8飞行控制中，WSINDY-MPC在噪声下实现更长预测时域、更低轨迹跟踪误差、更可靠避障和更低控制代价。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在噪声环境下仍能高效控制复杂非线性系统的数据驱动方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将WSINDYc与MPC结合，利用稀疏辨识获得简洁模型，从而在噪声下实现鲁棒控制。
idea_spark.risk: WSINDYc的辨识性能依赖于基函数选择和稀疏阈值，可能对高度非线性或快速时变系统适应性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 稀疏系统辨识与模型预测控制的结合为噪声环境下的实时控制提供了新思路，可启发其他数据驱动控制框架的改进。

en.tldr: Embedding WSINDy with actuation inputs into a model predictive control framework achieves more robust dynamics identification and superior control performance under noise.
en.motivation: Traditional control struggles with strong nonlinearities, noise, and computational constraints; data-driven methods offer fast, parsimonious, interpretable models but lack robustness to noise.
en.method: Proposes WSINDY-MPC framework, using WSINDYc for sparse system identification and integrating the identified model into model predictive control.
en.result: On tokamak plasma boundary, drone tracking/avoidance, chaotic Lorenz system, and F-8 flight control, WSINDY-MPC achieves longer prediction horizons, lower tracking error, more reliable obstacle clearance, and lower MPC cost under noise.
en.help_to_user: Provides a data-driven control method that remains effective for complex nonlinear systems under noisy conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Combining WSINDYc with MPC leverages sparse identification to obtain concise models for robust control under noise.
idea_spark.risk: WSINDYc's identification performance depends on basis function selection and sparsity thresholds, which may limit adaptability to highly nonlinear or rapidly time-varying systems.
idea_spark.inspiration: The fusion of sparse system identification and model predictive control offers a new paradigm for real-time control in noisy environments, inspiring improvements in other data-driven control frameworks.

papers.title: Scattering of the 3D Zakharov-Kuznetsov equation
papers.summary: We consider the Zakharov-Kuznetsov equation in space dimension 3: \[ \left\{ \begin{array}{l} \partial_t u + \partial_x Δu + \partial_x \frac{u^2}{2} = 0 \\ u(t = 0) = u_0 \end{array} \right. \] where $u : (t, x, y) \in \mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R} \times \mathbb{R}^2 \mapsto u(t, x, y) \in \mathbb{R}$, and $Δ= \partial_x^2 + Δ_y$ is the full Laplacian. We show that, for any $u_0$ satisfying \[ \Vert (1 + x^2 + |y|^2) u_0 \Vert_{H^1} \ll 1 \] then the global solution exhibits scattering in $H^1$. This is done using the method of space-time resonances, and more precisely the partial symmetries approach [GPW23] in order to treat the anisotropy. We introduce well suited anisotropic weighted norms, prove dispersive decay estimates adapted to these norms and an a priori estimate allowing to close by a bootstrap argument.

ai.tldr: 证明了三维Zakharov-Kuznetsov方程在小初值下的H¹散射性。
ai.motivation: 研究三维各向异性色散方程的散射行为。
ai.method: 采用时空共振法，结合部分对称性技巧，引入各向异性加权范数并建立色散衰减估计。
ai.result: 对满足加权H¹范数充分小的初值，整体解在H¹中散射。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解三维各向异性非线性色散方程的长时间行为提供了严格分析框架。

idea_spark.idea: 利用方程的部分对称性构造合适的加权范数，以处理各向异性带来的困难。
idea_spark.risk: 加权范数的选择高度依赖方程结构，推广时需重新验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将时空共振法与对称性降维结合，是处理高维各向异性问题的有效思路。

en.tldr: Proves H¹ scattering for the 3D Zakharov-Kuznetsov equation under small initial data.
en.motivation: To study the scattering behavior of anisotropic dispersive equations in three dimensions.
en.method: Uses the space-time resonance method with partial symmetries, introduces anisotropic weighted norms, and establishes dispersive decay estimates.
en.result: For initial data with sufficiently small weighted H¹ norm, the global solution scatters in H¹.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous analytical framework for understanding the long-time behavior of 3D anisotropic nonlinear dispersive equations.

idea_spark.idea: Exploit partial symmetries of the equation to design suitable weighted norms that overcome anisotropy difficulties.
idea_spark.risk: The choice of weighted norms is highly equation-specific and requires revalidation for generalizations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining space-time resonances with symmetry reduction is a promising strategy for high-dimensional anisotropic problems.

papers.title: Necessary and sufficient conditions for universality of Kolmogorov-Arnold networks
papers.summary: We analyze the universal approximation property of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) in terms of their edge functions. If these functions are all affine, then universality clearly fails. How many non-affine functions are needed, in addition to affine ones, to ensure universality? We show that a single one suffices. More precisely, we prove that deep KANs in which all edge functions are either affine or equal to a fixed continuous function $σ$ are dense in $C(K)$ for every compact set $K\subset\mathbb{R}^n$ if and only if $σ$ is non-affine. In contrast, for KANs with exactly two hidden layers, universality holds if and only if $σ$ is nonpolynomial. We further show that the full class of affine functions is not required; it can be replaced by a finite set without affecting universality. In particular, in the nonpolynomial case, a fixed family of five affine functions suffices when the depth is arbitrary. More generally, for every continuous non-affine function $σ$, there exists a finite affine family $A_σ$ such that deep KANs with edge functions in $A_σ\cup\{σ\}$ remain universal. We also prove that KANs with the spline-based edge parameterization introduced by Liu et al.~\cite{Liu2024} are universal approximators in the classical sense, even when the spline degree and knot sequence are fixed in advance.

ai.tldr: 本文分析了Kolmogorov-Arnold网络（KAN）的通用逼近性质，给出了边缘函数为仿射或固定连续函数时网络稠密的充要条件。
ai.motivation: 探究KAN中需要多少非仿射边缘函数才能保证通用逼近能力，以及仿射函数集是否可以缩减。
ai.method: 通过理论证明，分别针对深度KAN和两层KAN，建立非仿射或非多项式函数σ与通用逼近之间的充要条件，并构造有限仿射族。
ai.result: 深度KAN中一个非仿射σ即足以实现通用逼近；两层KAN中σ需为非多项式；仿射函数可缩减为有限集（如五个仿射函数）。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解KAN网络在何种条件下具备通用逼近能力，为网络设计和激活函数选择提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 探索使用更少的激活函数类型或固定参数化（如样条）来保持网络表达能力。
idea_spark.risk: 理论条件可能依赖于特定函数类，实际训练中非仿射函数的选择可能影响优化难度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受“一个非仿射函数即可”的结论启发，可尝试在KAN中仅使用一种非线性激活函数，其余均为线性，以简化实现并分析其表达能力。

en.tldr: This paper analyzes the universal approximation property of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) and provides necessary and sufficient conditions for density when edge functions are affine or a fixed continuous function.
en.motivation: To determine how many non-affine edge functions are needed for universality in KANs and whether the affine class can be reduced.
en.method: Theoretical proofs establishing necessary and sufficient conditions for deep and two-hidden-layer KANs based on non-affine or nonpolynomial σ, and constructing finite affine families.
en.result: A single non-affine σ suffices for deep KANs; σ must be nonpolynomial for two-layer KANs; the affine class can be replaced by a finite set (e.g., five affine functions).
en.help_to_user: Provides theoretical guidance on when KANs are universal approximators, aiding network design and activation function selection.

idea_spark.idea: Explore using fewer activation types or fixed parameterizations (e.g., splines) to maintain expressiveness.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical conditions may depend on specific function classes; practical training with non-affine functions could affect optimization difficulty.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by 'one non-affine function suffices', one could try using only one nonlinear activation in KANs and keep the rest linear to simplify implementation and analyze expressiveness.

papers.title: A Functorial Formulation of Neighborhood Aggregating Deep Learning
papers.summary: We provide a mathematical interpretation of convolutional (or message passing) neural networks by using presheaves and copresheaves of the set of continuous functions over a topological space. Based on this interpretation, we formulate a theoretical heuristic which elaborates a number of empirical limitations of these neural networks by using obstructions on such sets of continuous functions over a topological space to be sheaves or copresheaves.

ai.tldr: 用预层和余层解释卷积/消息传递神经网络，揭示其理论局限。
ai.motivation: 为邻域聚合深度学习（如卷积网络、消息传递网络）提供严格的数学基础，并解释其经验局限。
ai.method: 将连续函数集上的预层和余层作为数学框架，通过拓扑空间上的障碍条件分析网络能力。
ai.result: 提出了一个理论启发式，阐明了这些网络在成为层或余层时存在的障碍，对应其经验局限。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解邻域聚合网络的理论边界，指导更合理的网络设计。

idea_spark.idea: 利用层论中的障碍条件设计新的网络结构，以突破现有局限。
idea_spark.risk: 理论结果可能过于抽象，难以直接转化为实用算法。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将拓扑障碍作为网络设计约束，可能催生更强大的图神经网络变体。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将拓扑障碍作为网络设计约束，可能催生更强大的图神经网络变体。

en.tldr: Interprets convolutional/message-passing neural networks using presheaves and copresheaves, revealing theoretical limitations.
en.motivation: To provide a rigorous mathematical foundation for neighborhood aggregating deep learning (e.g., CNNs, message-passing networks) and explain their empirical limitations.
en.method: Uses presheaves and copresheaves of continuous function sets over a topological space as a framework, analyzing network capabilities via obstructions to being sheaves or copresheaves.
en.result: Proposes a theoretical heuristic that identifies obstructions preventing these networks from being sheaves or copresheaves, corresponding to empirical limitations.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the theoretical boundaries of neighborhood aggregating networks, guiding more principled network design.

idea_spark.idea: Design novel network architectures using sheaf-theoretic obstruction conditions to overcome current limitations.
idea_spark.risk: Theoretical results may be too abstract to translate directly into practical algorithms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using topological obstructions as design constraints could lead to more powerful graph neural network variants.

papers.title: Intervention-Aware Multiscale Representation Learning from Imaging Phenomics and Perturbation Transcriptomics
papers.summary: Microscopy-based phenotypic profiling is scalable for drug discovery but lacks the mechanistic depth of transcriptomics, which remains costly and scarce. Existing multimodal approaches either use images to support other modalities or naively align representations by sample identity, ignoring cell-type and dose variations in weakly paired data-limiting generalization to unseen interventions. In this paper, we introduce an intervention-aware distillation framework that leverages perturbational transcriptomics to guide image representation learning. A transcriptome-conditioned teacher integrates gene expression and intervention metadata to produce soft distributions over a chemistry-aware codebook organized by drug similarity. The teacher employs a fine-tuned single-cell foundation model to encode cell-type context and disentangle dose effects. An image-only student learns to predict these distributions from microscopy alone, distilling mechanistic knowledge while operating independently at test time. This design emphasizes intervention semantics rather than identity alignment and explicitly handles dose and cell-type mismatches. We provide theoretical guarantees showing that transcriptomic guidance tightens the risk bound for image-based prediction. On Cell Painting and RxRx datasets paired with L1000, our method significantly improves one-shot transfer to unseen interventions and drug-target gene discovery compared to self-supervised and alignment baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出一种干预感知蒸馏框架，利用扰动转录组学指导图像表示学习，解决弱配对数据中细胞类型和剂量变化导致的泛化问题。
ai.motivation: 基于显微镜的表型分析可扩展但缺乏转录组学的机制深度，而转录组学成本高且数据稀缺；现有多模态方法忽略细胞类型和剂量变化，泛化能力差。
ai.method: 构建转录组条件教师模型，整合基因表达和干预元数据，通过化学感知码本生成软分布；学生模型仅从显微镜图像预测这些分布，实现独立推理。
ai.result: 在Cell Painting和RxRx数据集上，相比自监督和对齐基线，显著提升对未见干预的一次性迁移和药物靶点基因发现。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效利用稀缺转录组数据增强图像表型分析的方法，适用于药物筛选和靶点发现。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将化学感知码本替换为基于知识图谱的码本，以捕获更丰富的药物关系。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖预训练单细胞基础模型，可能限制在无此类模型领域的应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用干预语义而非身份对齐的思路，可启发其他生物医学多模态学习任务。

zh.motivation: 基于显微镜的表型分析可扩展但缺乏转录组学的机制深度，而转录组学成本高且数据稀缺；现有多模态方法忽略细胞类型和剂量变化，泛化能力差。
zh.method: 构建转录组条件教师模型，整合基因表达和干预元数据，通过化学感知码本生成软分布；学生模型仅从显微镜图像预测这些分布，实现独立推理。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种高效利用稀缺转录组数据增强图像表型分析的方法，适用于药物筛选和靶点发现。

en.tldr: We propose an intervention-aware distillation framework that leverages perturbation transcriptomics to guide image representation learning, addressing generalization issues caused by cell-type and dose variations in weakly paired data.
en.motivation: Microscopy-based phenotypic profiling is scalable but lacks mechanistic depth of transcriptomics, which is costly and scarce; existing multimodal methods ignore cell-type and dose variations, limiting generalization.
en.method: A transcriptome-conditioned teacher integrates gene expression and intervention metadata to produce soft distributions over a chemistry-aware codebook; an image-only student learns to predict these distributions from microscopy alone.
en.result: On Cell Painting and RxRx datasets, our method significantly improves one-shot transfer to unseen interventions and drug-target gene discovery over self-supervised and alignment baselines.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient way to augment image-based phenotypic profiling with scarce transcriptomic data, useful for drug screening and target discovery.

idea_spark.idea: Explore replacing the chemistry-aware codebook with a knowledge graph-based codebook to capture richer drug relationships.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on a pre-trained single-cell foundation model, which may limit application in domains without such models.
idea_spark.inspiration: The focus on intervention semantics rather than identity alignment can inspire other biomedical multimodal learning tasks.

papers.title: Intermittency-Driven Turbulence Cascade Memory Extends the Markov-Einstein Coherence Length Beyond the Canonical Estimate
papers.summary: Using direct numerical simulation of forced isotropic turbulence at $\text{Re}_λ\approx 1300$ and $\approx 433$, together with two independent Markov-by-construction null surrogates, we measure the Markov--Einstein coherence length of the turbulent energy cascade to be $Δr \approx 3.2$-$3.6$ in log-scale cascade coordinates, approximately three times the canonical estimate $Δr \approx 1$. Stratifying the gap-scan test by local dissipation intensity and by increment amplitude reveals that intermittent events carry $Δr \approx 3$-$4$, while at mid-inertial-range scales the quiescent cascade recovers $Δr \approx 1.0$-$1.4$, consistent with the canonical value. Near the dissipation range this pattern reverses: bulk dynamics carry more memory than extreme events, consistent with the spectral bottleneck. The excess memory is internal to the inertial range and Reynolds-number-independent over $\text{Re}_λ\approx 433$-$1300$. These findings indicate that the Markov approximation underlying the cascade Fokker-Planck equation and fluctuation-theorem analyses is substantially more restrictive than previously assumed, and that a non-Markovian correction, informed by the amplitude-dependent memory structure identified here, is needed for the intermittent component of the cascade.

ai.tldr: 通过直接数值模拟发现，湍流能量级联的马尔可夫-爱因斯坦相干长度约为规范估计的三倍，间歇事件携带更长记忆，而平静级联恢复规范值，表明马尔可夫近似需非马尔可夫修正。
ai.motivation: 检验湍流能量级联中马尔可夫近似的有效性，并测量马尔可夫-爱因斯坦相干长度是否超出传统估计。
ai.method: 对强迫各向同性湍流进行直接数值模拟（Reλ≈1300和433），使用马尔可夫构造的零假设替代数据，并通过局部耗散强度和增量振幅分层分析间隙扫描测试。
ai.result: 相干长度Δr≈3.2-3.6，约为规范估计的三倍；间歇事件携带Δr≈3-4，平静级联恢复Δr≈1.0-1.4；耗散区模式反转，且记忆与雷诺数无关。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解湍流级联中记忆效应的本质，指导非马尔可夫修正模型的构建。

idea_spark.idea: 基于间歇事件记忆长度，设计非马尔可夫修正的Fokker-Planck方程以更准确描述湍流级联。
idea_spark.risk: 非马尔可夫修正可能增加模型复杂度，且参数化需大量高分辨率数据验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 间歇事件携带更长记忆的发现启发将湍流视为具有长程相关性的随机过程，类似某些气候或金融时间序列。
